page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.6xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance firmly in the mid-range for general compute and memory tasks. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1884.19 and a multi-core score of 23298.44. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1259.99 GB/sec, which drops to 129.31 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 63.06 GB/sec. In cryptographic and integer operations, the CPU utilizes hardware-accelerated instructions such as AVX-512 and AES, achieving a PassMark CPU encryption score of 21932.51.

For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, achieving 298.93 tokens per second on a 135M parameter model. However, larger model inference is constrained, with the 7B model processing prompts at 48.11 tokens per second. The server is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local storage, such as software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.6xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, this instance provides a 2.0 GB per core resource density, making it highly suitable for CPU-bound tasks. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate solid mid-range performance, highlighted by a multi-core Geekbench score of 11562.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 29354.86. The instance excels in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and static web serving, while also showing strong text generation capabilities for small-scale language models. This hardware profile offers a balanced, cost-efficient solution for dedicated compute workloads that do not require local disk storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance CPU workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, delivering balanced compute performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 24-core compute-optimized instance designed for high-throughput processing, software compilation, and network-dependent workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Strong performance in multi-threaded decompression and software compilation
Optimized for compute-intensive workloads, static web serving, and small LLM inference
